name: Keep the restored correct surface and let only half the CTA issue hot-band Pg2s async copies

summary.name: Keep the restored correct surface and let only half the CTA issue hot-band Pg2s async copies
summary.hypothesis: The light L2 swizzle was basically flat, which leaves the user's Pg2s/staging idea as the next low-risk family. The current hot-band kernel still has all 256 threads participate in both A and B staging, even though tensor activity remains around 38.6% and the main kernel is not obviously DRAM bound. A conservative first step is to keep the tile shape and consumer math unchanged but have only the first 128 threads issue the cp.async staging loops for the hot-band kernel. That tests whether reducing staging-side scheduler pressure helps without taking the correctness risks of a true producer-only warp split.
summary.expected_bottleneck: CTA-level staging orchestration and LSU/shared issue pressure during Pg2s rather than warp-local MMA scheduling.
summary.risk: Moderate. The copy count stays identical, but a poorer producer subset could just add loop overhead without reducing the real bottleneck.
summary.stop_condition: Reject if tensor activity stays flat and mio/barrier do not improve, or if the staging subset regresses runtime.

notes: Build passed. Node A must measure this implementation next.
